results.prompt: Build a one-page neon fitness landing page I can preview. It must include the exact heading Kinetic Pulse, a hero paragraph, and a primary CTA button labeled Start Training. Use a dark visual style and make it runnable now.

dom.title: kinetic pulse
dom.text:
K
kinetic pulse
Platform
Preview
Flow
Launch
View programs

NEON PERFORMANCE TRAINING

kinetic pulse

A dark, neon fitness landing page for athletes who want sharp programming, visible momentum, and a training flow that feels ready to start.

start training
Explore the training flow
6 wk
strength cycle preview
24/7
training plan access
4.9/5
athlete energy rating

WHY IT FEELS BETTER

Electric energy, clear programs, and a CTA that feels ready to move.

The page keeps the fitness promise visible: powerful headline, performance proof, and direct training actions without burying the user in generic marketing copy.

Neon dark shell
Training-first CTA
Mobile workout flow

FLOW

Pulse sessions

High-output training blocks with enough structure to feel serious without becoming intimidating.

FLOW

Recovery rhythm

A calmer supporting section balances the neon intensity with mobility, rest, and repeatable progress.

FLOW

Coach-ready preview

Responsive cards, strong CTA grouping, and a training story that still reads clearly on a phone.

READY TO MOVE

Turn intent into the next training block.
start training

results.prompt: Build the first runnable version now. Create a compact but polished shared shopping app for a household or roommates. Use Tailwind CSS v4 styling and framer-motion for subtle motion, seed mock data for members, items, aisle or category groupings, and activity messages. The preview must visibly include the heading Shared Shopping List plus separate sections labeled Household and Activity Chat.

dom.title: Shared Shopping List
dom.text:
FIRST RUNNABLE VERSION NOW. CREATE A COMPACT BUT POLISHED SHARED SHOPPING APP FOR A HOUSEHOLD OR ROOMMATES. USE TAILWIND CSS V4 STYLING AND FRAMER-MOTION FOR SUBTLE MOTION, SEED MOCK DATA FOR MEMBERS, ITEMS, AISLE OR CATEGORY GROUPINGS, AND ACTIVITY MESSAGES. THE PREVIEW MUST VISIBLY INCLUDE THE HEADING SHARED SHOPPING LIST PLUS SEPARATE SECTIONS LABELED HOUSEHOLD AND ACTIVITY CHAT.

Shared Shopping List

Built for a real store run: household context on one side, aisle-first list in the middle, and a route-ready store plan that feels like a product instead of a dashboard.

results.prompt: Build a custom storefront app for a premium home goods brand. It needs catalog, product detail, cart summary, and checkout-ready flow in the first preview.

dom.title: Custom Storefront
dom.text:
CUSTOM STOREFRONT APP FOR A PREMIUM HOME GOODS BRAND. IT NEEDS CATALOG, PRODUCT DETAIL, CART SUMMARY, AND CHECKOUT-READY FLOW IN THE FIRST PREVIEW.

Custom storefront, not a borrowed demo shell.

This first pass focuses on the real commerce loop: discover products, inspect the detail view, add to cart, and reach a believable checkout summary without the app feeling like a generic starter.

Category browsing that reads like a real store
Product detail, price, and shipping cues in one surface
A cart summary that updates immediately without backend wiring yet
BUILDER TARGET
1 item in cart

Use this as the first storefront shape before adding real inventory, search, variants, or payments.

CATEGORIES
6

$46

results.prompt: Build an analytics dashboard with charts, revenue over time, traffic sources, KPI cards, and date range filters.
results.response:
Building a React analytics dashboard with Recharts. ```json title="package.json" { "name": "dashboard-app", "private": true, "version": "0.0.0", "type": "module", "scripts": { "dev": "vite", "build": "vite build" }, "dependencies": { "react": "^18.3.1", "react-dom": "^18.3.1", "recharts": "^2.12.7" }, "devDependencies": { "@types/react": "^18", "@types/react-dom": "^18", "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.1", "typescript": "^5", "vite": "^5.4.1" } } ``` ```tsx title="src/App.tsx" 'use client'; import { AreaChart, Area, BarChart, Bar, PieChart, Pie, Cell, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const revenueData = [ { month: 'Jan', revenue: 4200, users: 240 }, { month: 'Feb', revenue: 5800, users: 310 }, { month: 'Mar', revenue: 5200, users: 290 }, { month: 'Apr', revenue: 7100, users: 430 }, { month: 'May', revenue: 8400, users: 510 }, { month: 'Jun', revenue: 9200, users: 580 }, ]; const trafficData = [ { name: 'Organic', value: 45 }, { name: 'Direct', value: 28 }, { name: 'Social', value: 18 }, { name: 'Referral', value: 9 }, ]; const COLORS = ['#6366f1', '#22d3ee', '#f59e0b', '#10b981']; const statCards = [ { label: 'Total Revenue', value: '$39,900', change: '+12%' }, { label: 'Active Users', value: '2,360', change: '+8%' }, { label: 'Conversion', value: '3.4%', change: '+0.6%' }, { label: 'Churn Rate', value: '1.2%', change: '-0.3%' }, ]; export default function Dashboard() { return (

Analytics Dashboard

{statCards.map(c => (
{c.label}
{c.value}
{c.change} vs last month
))}
{['Last 7 days', 'Last 30 days', 'Last 90 days', 'Year to date'].map((range) => { const active = range === 'Last 30 days'; return ( ); })}

Revenue Over Time

Monthly Users

Traffic Sources

{trafficData.map((_, i) => )}
); } ``` ```tsx title="src/main.tsx" import React from 'react'; import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client'; import App from './App'; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root') as HTMLElement).render( ); ``` ```html title="index.html" Dashboard
``` ```js title="vite.config.js" import { defineConfig } from 'vite'; import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [react()] }); ``` **Run:** `npm install && npm run dev` Charts included: Area (revenue trend), Bar (monthly users), Pie (traffic sources). Add more by importing from `recharts`.

results.prompt: Build a Rust CLI incident triage tool with clap commands. Return Cargo.toml and src/main.rs as separate complete files.
warnings: project update artifact not observed before file polling: Timed out waiting for project update artifact

contents.Cargo.toml:
[package]
name = "rust-cli"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
